Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: v1.6.3 — Russia/Soviet era naming, anachronistic countries, era messages

Russia naming (WorldCountriesDatabase + GameState):
- Pre-1917: "Russian Empire" (Tsarist autocracy, non-aligned, 0 nukes,
  era-scaled GDP $0.05T–$0.25T and military strength)
- 1917–1921: "Russia" (Revolution/Civil War, unstable, stab=15)
- 1922–1991: "Soviet Union" as before (communist, eastern, nuclear 1949+)
- Fixed ussr() flag: was "🇺🇸" (US flag!) — now correctly "🇷🇺"

Anachronistic countries removed from pre-founding eras (countriesForYear):
- Saudi Arabia removed pre-1932 (Kingdom unified 1932)
- South Korea removed pre-1945 (post-Japanese surrender)
- North Korea removed pre-1948
- Pakistan removed pre-1947 (partition of India)
- Jordan removed pre-1946
- Singapore removed pre-1965
- UAE, Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait removed pre-1971
- ~44 post-colonial African nations removed pre-1960
- Timor-Leste removed pre-2002, Kosovo removed pre-2008

Nuclear status cleanup in countriesForYear:
- All declared/undeclared nuclear status cleared before confirmed first test
  (USA 1945, USSR 1949, UK 1952, France 1960, China 1964, etc.)
- Saudi Arabia .suspected cleared pre-2000
- Iran .developing cleared pre-1985

Era-appropriate diplomatic messages (SafeDiplomacyService):
- Pre-modern turns: period-correct language ("We propose a formal treaty of
  friendship and commerce") instead of "$5B economic aid"
- Modern turns: retain contemporary financial/diplomatic framing

primaryThreatLabel: expanded to cover all eras accurately
- "European Powers" (pre-1815), "Civil War" (1861–65),
  "Rising Fascism" (1922–39), "Axis Powers" (1939–45), etc.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use S&P 500 index (^GSPC) as benchmark instead of SPY ETF

SPY is an ETF with ~0.09% expense ratio and tracking error.
The correct benchmark is the S&P 500 index (^GSPC) itself.

Changes:
- Dashboard API: all benchmark calculations now use ^GSPC
- Live engine: _get_spy_close() fetches ^GSPC
- spy_benchmark.csv: regenerated with ^GSPC data (2000-2026)
- cycle_log.json: updated spy_start/spy_end to ^GSPC prices
- Labels: "S&P 500 (SPY)" -> "S&P 500" everywhere
- JS: "Superando/Detrás de SPY" -> "S&P 500"

Note: SPY is still used for regime filters (SMA200, vol targeting)
which is appropriate — regime detection doesn't need index precision.

commit.message:
Add QRZ status indicator, configurable API URL, and error log

- Add #hdr-qrz badge to logger header (green/muted/red) reflecting
  whether QRZ auth and lookups are succeeding; updates after each
  lookup and after QRZ backfill
- Clicking the badge opens a QrzLogModal showing timestamped errors
  (newest first); notifies if QRZ is not configured
- QRZClient tracks status via _mark_ok()/_log_error() — "not found"
  responses count as OK (valid auth); network/auth failures are logged
- Add qrz_api_url config field ([qrz] api_url) with settings UI row,
  defaulting to https://xmldata.qrz.com/xml/current/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: drop duplicate parse errors with incorrect offsets (#711)

## Summary

Closes #235

- apollo-compiler's parse errors are duplicates of apollo-parser's (both
catch the same syntax mistakes), but were hardcoded to `offset: 0`,
causing spurious diagnostics at line 1
- Drops the duplicate error collection in both `parse_graphql` and
`extract_and_parse`, keeping only apollo-parser errors which have
correct byte offsets
- Validation/semantic errors are unaffected -- they use a separate code
path (`analysis::validation`) that correctly uses `line_column_range()`

## Test plan

- [x] All existing `graphql-syntax` tests pass (28/28)
- [x] Verified via temporary test that apollo-compiler parse errors are
exact duplicates of apollo-parser errors for syntax mistakes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

commit.message:
Fix Siigo payment total rounding mismatch

Recalculate total from base+tax to match Siigo's internal rounding.
3900000/1.19 = 3277310.92, tax = 622689.07, total = 3899999.99
Payment value must match this recalculated total, not the original amount.

repository.description: Sistema de gestión para agencias de marketing

commit.message:
Fix price scrambling: use Ticker.history() instead of yf.download()

yf.download() shares a session cache that causes cross-ticker data
contamination when fetching multiple symbols sequentially. Switch to
yf.Ticker().history() which uses per-ticker sessions and returns
flat column indexes (no MultiIndex issues).

commit.message:
Replace orbit line raycaster with screen-space hit testing

The THREE.js Line raycaster was unreliable — threshold scaling never
felt right at varying distances. New approach projects each orbit's
center+radius to screen pixels and checks if the mouse is within 4px
of the circle edge. Much more predictable and zero lag.

Chunk replace-source uploads to 200 sites per request (fix 413)

Splits large uploads into 200-site chunks to stay under nginx's
default 1MB body limit. First chunk uses replace-source (snapshot +
wipe + insert), subsequent chunks use batch-upload with
create_snapshot=false to append.

Location fixes:
- loc-empty-btn: larger pill hit area (dashed border, padding) for easier clicking
- openLocDropdown: URL normalization fallback (trailing slash + case) so dash
  contacts always open their dropdown
- _locTypeahead: typed text prepended as first suggestion if not in API results,
  enabling country-only selections like 'Germany'
- confirmLocation: fix normalization bug — only accept suggestion if it matches
  the typed text exactly (case-insensitive), preventing 'Germany' → 'Berlin, Germany'
- _locBuildActionBtns: Google search now includes LinkedIn URL + 'business' keyword
  for more targeted location lookup

Fix sidebar scroll on Tk 9/macOS using <TouchpadScroll> event

Tk 9 on macOS uses <TouchpadScroll> instead of <MouseWheel> for
trackpad scrolling. Routes scroll events to sidebar or canvas based
on mouse position. Also redirects mouse wheel <MouseWheel> events
from canvas to sidebar when cursor is over it. Moves header to a
fixed non-scrolling frame above the sidebar.

commit.message:
feat(windows): upgrade Tier 3 (Pro) default model to Qwen3-30B-A3B MoE (#53)

Qwen3-30B-A3B is a 30B Mixture-of-Experts model with only 3B active
parameters per token. At Q4_K_M quantization (17.3GB), it fits
comfortably in 20-24GB VRAM cards while delivering better quality
than the dense 14B model it replaces.

Changes:
- Tier 3 (20-39GB VRAM): qwen3-14b → qwen3-30b-a3b
- Fixed GgufFile casing to match HuggingFace (Qwen3-30B-A3B-Q4_K_M.gguf)
  across Tier 3, Tier 4, and SH_COMPACT
- Updated ConvertTo-ModelFromTier lookup for Tier 3

Tier 3 keeps MaxContext=32768 (conservative for 24GB cards).
Tier 4 (40GB+) gets the same model but with MaxContext=131072.

Tested on RTX 5090 Laptop GPU (24GB VRAM, Blackwell sm_120).

commit.message:
Fix stitched image naming using project folder instead of user name

In bounded acquisition with an existing project open, stitched images
were named after the project folder (e.g., JN167_re-coverslip) instead
of the user-entered sample name from the dialog. The metadata.sampleName
field was being set from the path-based sampleName parameter instead of
sample.sampleName() (the user's display name).

Now performRegionStitching uses sample.sampleName() for the display
name in StitchingMetadata while keeping sampleName for path construction.
